And so obviously don’t get to do it on the actual day, but having a few extra days off where I can get away and be around family was definitely cool.”With a new toddler at home, it seems Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes are fully focused on their family as the star player works on his recovery, ahead of the new season.

Article Title: NFL trade rumors: NFL insider links Green Bay Packers to $27M ex New Orleans Saints Pro Bowler as he breaks records
Article Text:
NFL insider links Green Bay Packers to $27M ex New Orleans Saints Pro Bowler as he breaks records.(Image via X)

NFL insider suggests the Green Bay Packers might be Cameron Jordan’s “perfect” landing spot

The Green Bay Packers started strong but ended the season with 9 wins and 8 losses, resulting in a second place finish in the NFC North.As the Green Bay Packers gear up for the new season, popular NFL insider Jason La Canfora suggests that the team could be eyeing the former New Orleans Saints’ star player.A few hours ago, NFL insider Jason La Canfora, spoke on Casino Guru about the Green Bay Packers being a “perfect” fit for Cameron Jordan.Jason La Canfora said, “Jordan is going to Canton, Ohio, one day. He is coming off a 10.5-sack season and after spending years in the abyss with the Saints, he is driven by a ring above all else…”The popular NFL insider added, “At age 37, this might be it. Green Bay badly needs fortification to its line, and with Micah Parsons injured for the first half of the season, having Jordan to move around inside and outside would be huge.”Jason La Canfora continued, “He’s opined about preferring to play for a warm-weather team … but money talks.”However, the Green Bay Packers have stayed distant from the trade rumors about the eight time Pro Bowler, Cameron Jordan.After being drafted in the NFL by the New Orleans Saints, Cameron Jordan remained with the team throughout his fifteen year old career.Although he is a free agent after his recent contract with the Saints expired at the end of the 2025 season, there have been rumors about Cameron Jordan returning to the team.Back in December 2025, Cameron Jordan opened up about his situation with the Saints as he said, “I know what I want to do, what I want to accomplish. If that doesn’t line up (with the Saints), that’s fine as well…”He added, “Just because I love the city and the organization, doesn’t mean the organization or the city has to love me back. Article Title: Taylor Swift prioritizes major life decision as ‘Showgirl Tour’ rumors and Travis Kelce wedding date surface

Insiders reveal Taylor Swift’s real priority as ‘Showgirl Tour’ rumors swirl (Getty Images)

Taylor Swift’s priority shifts as wedding plans take center stage

The date is set, the plans are real, and the biggest question around Taylor Swift right now has little to do with music. As speculation about a quick return to touring gathers pace, fresh details point in a very different direction. Swift and Travis Kelce are quietly building toward a June 13, 2026 wedding, and those close to the couple insist the months ahead are not about stadium lights or setlists. They are about something far more personal, and far more intentional.Talk of a new tour has followed Taylor Swift ever since her Eras run redefined global pop success. But insiders are pushing back on that narrative. “She does have another record in the works, but there’s no pressure to release it anytime soon. It’s still very much in the creative stages,” insiders said to DailyMail, noting Swift has no plans for a Life of a Showgirl tour. “She never meant to do a massive tour attached to it. ”That tone feels deliberate. After years of relentless output, Swift appears to be choosing pause over pace. Friends like Selena Gomez and Gigi Hadid are expected to stand beside her as bridesmaids, along with Blake Lively and Sophie Turner, signaling just how intimate and personal the celebration is shaping up to be.And the message from inside her circle is consistent. “Taylor’s priority right now is her personal life.She and Travis are saving all their energy for the big summer they have coming up.” It is a rare shift for an artist whose schedule rarely allowed for stillness. This time, the stillness seems intentional.There is another layer to the timing, and it ties closely to Travis Kelce’s calendar. With the Kansas City Chiefs expected to begin preparations for the new season in late July, the couple’s window for uninterrupted time is narrow. That urgency is shaping their plans for wedding.“They have been planning this for weeks and have already informed friends and family that they will be ‘off the grid’ during this time,” insiders said. “Unless there is an emergency, they want to limit outside contact to truly immerse themselves in this once-in-a-lifetime experience before Travis returns to the football field.”That detail lands differently when placed alongside their timeline. From a quiet relationship reveal in 2023 to an engagement announced on Aug. 26, 2025, the pace has been steady but private. Swift’s caption at the time captured that balance perfectly: “Your English teacher and your gym teacher are getting married 🧨”.Now, with loved ones told to keep their summer flexible and details tightly held, the direction feels clear. The wedding will come before football. The music can wait. And for once, the spotlight is not something Taylor Swift seems eager to chase.

Article Title: "She’s having sleepless nights": Taylor Swift considers changing wedding plans as pressure builds ahead of big day with Travis Kelce
Article Text:
Taylor Swift is reportedly facing mounting stress while planning her wedding with Travis Kelce, as tight scheduling, public leaks, and complex logistics take a toll. At one point, she even considered changing plans to maintain privacy. Despite sleepless nights and pressure, the couple remains focused on creating an intimate, meaningful ceremony while balancing Kelce’s NFL commitments and Swift’s vision.

Taylor Swift reportedly rethinks wedding plans amid mounting pressure and leaks

Poll What aspect of the wedding do you think is most important for Taylor Swift? Privacy Tradition Spectacle

Wedding bells may be getting closer for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce , but behind the excitement lies a growing sense of pressure. With a summer ceremony being discussed and the NFL calendar looming, the timeline has quietly turned into a source of tension. What should feel magical is now a juggling act between privacy, perfection, and busy schedules.People say that Swift, who is known for her careful planning and love of detail, is feeling the stress of it all. The challenge is not just organizing a wedding. It is doing so while the world watches, speculates, and leaks details at every turn. That loss of control appears to be one of the biggest stress points.Sources suggest that while Swift is deeply excited, “the logistics of it all are causing a ton of stress.” The scale of the event, combined with constant public attention, has pushed her to rethink parts of the plan. At one stage, she even “floated the idea of changing their plans completely to throw everything off and avoid having it turn into a circus.”That instinct shows that she wants to protect the moment. Rhode Island has become a likely place because it is both familiar and private. A combination of her own property and a nearby high-end resort could host the party, giving everyone room without losing closeness.Swift is mostly in charge of planning now. “Travis handed off most of the planning to Taylor because she has a real vision of what she wants,” a source said. “The downside of that is she’s getting very overwhelmed, to the point she’s having sleepless nights as a result.” That emotional toll highlights just how much care she is putting into every detail.Despite the stress, the couple appears aligned in what matters most. Kelce has openly spoken about respecting Swift’s career, and that mutual understanding seems to guide their decisions. The guest list reflects that balance too. Close friends such as Selena Gomez , Gigi Hadid, and Emma Stone are expected, alongside Kelce’s inner circle, including Patrick Mahomes and his brother Jason Kelce.The ceremony itself is expected to lean traditional, with personal touches woven throughout. Swift’s approach remains clear. Keep it meaningful. Keep it close. Even with backup plans in place, her focus is not on spectacle. It is on creating a moment that feels real, even in the spotlight.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift is reportedly facing mounting stress while planning her wedding with Travis Kelce, as tight scheduling, public leaks, and complex logistics take a toll. At one point, she even considered changing plans to maintain privacy. Despite sleepless nights and pressure, the couple remains focused on creating an intimate, meaningful ceremony.

Article Title: Lebron James Stephen A Smith: NFL star tears into Stephen A. Smith after basketball legend LeBron James' Memphis comments spark debate
Article Text:
NFL star tears into Stephen A. Smith after basketball legend LeBron James' Memphis comments spark debate (Getty)

NFL legend Terrell Owens escalates LeBron debate as Stephen A. Smith draws fire





The LeBron James Memphis debate just took a sharp turn. What began as a blunt take from the NBA star has now dragged in NFL voices. After a lot of chatter, NFL legend Terrell Owens jumped in and made sure the noise got louder.This is no longer just about Memphis or the Grizzlies. It is now about who gets to speak, who gets called out, and how far that criticism can go. And Owens did not hold back for a second.On his Insta story, NFL star Terrell Owens slammed Smith. Owens wrote, “I told y’all this n— was a clown and ain’t about 💩.” It was the direct shot at Smith.The spark came earlier this week when LeBron spoke on the Bob Does Sports YouTube show. He took a swipe at Memphis, saying he would not enjoy being there at this stage of his career and even suggested the team should move to Nashville. He also joked he might have “pulled an Eli Manning” back in 2003That remark has caught widespread attention. But things really heated up the next day. Then, Stephen A. Smith addressed it on ESPN. He pushed back hard.“Because I ain’t trying to let nobody off the hook on that. I wasn’t dressed in shorts, lolligagging on the golf course with a bunch of peeps that don’t look anything like the folks that reside in Memphis. That’s where it was foul.”When I spoke, I spoke about what this city needs in order to look out for the vast majority of its citizens who happen to be Black and are living there.I did it right here, on the mic with the cameras rolling. Dead serious as hell,” Smith exclaimedl, Smith said, pointing to Memphis’ community and economic impact.That argument did not land well everywhere. Also, former NBA player Matt Barnes fired back on Instagram. He questioned Smith’s stance and called out what he saw as selective criticism. Barnes said, “You’ve been the same man… disrespecting Serena Williams… but you got an issue with LeBron.”The reaction also ties back to history. Owens had earlier taken aim at Smith on the All The Smoke podcast, accusing him of twisting stories and pushing a negative narrative. That tension clearly has not cooled.Now the story has layers. LeBron’s Memphis take lit the fuse. Smith tried to frame the bigger picture. Barnes questioned his credibility. And Owens? He poured fuel on the fire. At this point, this is not just a debate. It is a full-blown clash across leagues, voices, and reputations. And with names this big involved, it is only getting louder.
Article Summary: LeBron James took a swipe at Memphis and suggested the Grizzlies move to Nashville. NBA legend Stephen A. Smith addressed the issue on ESPN. NFL legend Terrell Owens took aim at Smith on his Insta story. Matt Barnes questioned Smith's credibility and called out selective criticism.

Article Title: Is there a Plan B in Pittsburgh? Steelers reportedly unprepared if Aaron Rodgers walks away before 2026 season
Article Text:
Aaron Rodgers future with Steelers is uncertain (Getty Images)

Steelers wait on Aaron Rodgers as draft pressure builds



Will Howard , Ty Simpson, and the search for a fallback



With the 2026 NFL Draft just weeks away, the Pittsburgh Steelers still does not know who will lead the offense. Aaron Rodgers remains undecided about returning, despite a steady 2025 season that saw him throw for 3,322 yards with 24 touchdowns. The clock is ticking, and the franchise appears to be bracing for more than one outcome.Aaron Rodgers gave Pittsburgh stability last season. He was efficient, careful with the ball, and rarely put the team in difficult spots. Yet that consistency has not brought clarity this spring.Owner Art Rooney II made it clear the team does not want this uncertainty dragging into late April. “I have not talked to him. Coach [McCarthy] has been in contact with him pretty regularly,” Rooney told ESPN’s Brooke Pryor. “I think a decision is probably coming soon.”That timeline matters. The draft begins April 23, and the Steelers would prefer a firm answer before they are on the clock.There is a quiet tension building inside the organization. If Rodgers returns, the plan holds. If he does not, Pittsburgh must pivot quickly, possibly without a clear-cut starter in place.According to ESPN’s Brooke Pryor, that pivot could include adding a young arm.“I think it’s a very real possibility that they could draft a quarterback, maybe not in the first round,” Pryor said. “They have other needs to address, but a later-round guy.”That approach suggests caution. It also hints at a team not fully convinced about its current depth.The Steelers are not starting from zero. Will Howard, now in his second year, has drawn internal support. Mike McCarthy has spoken positively about his development.“I’m really excited about Will Howard,” McCarthy said. “He’s someone that, I thought, he really came on there at Ohio State.”Still, optimism does not always equal certainty.That is where other names enter the conversation. Ty Simpson has quietly built a case as a potential option. His numbers stand out, 3,567 yards, 28 touchdowns, and just five interceptions last season. More importantly, evaluators see traits that translate.ESPN analyst Louis Riddick did not hold back. “I like Will Howard – I don’t like Will Howard as much as Ty Simpson. I don’t even think they’re in the same stratosphere as far as their ability to throw the football. I think Ty Simpson is a guy who, right now, based upon how the season ended – and we’ve talked about it a lot and we’re going to be talking about it a lot more,” Riddick said, via On3. “Based upon how the latter half of the season ended, which wasn’t all on him. The guy was banged up, offensive line is very inconsistent, the running game is nonexistent and the wide receiver corps was up-and-down from week to week. And then, he turns it around in the playoffs against Oklahoma and brings his team back from a 17-point deficit. He’s tailor-made for Mike McCarthy’s system – and Mike has coached some good quarterbacks.”That endorsement carries weight. It also raises a simple question. How long can Pittsburgh afford to wait?For now, everything circles back to Rodgers. His decision will shape the draft, the depth chart, and perhaps the entire season. Until then, the Steelers remain in limbo, balancing patience with urgency as one answer holds up every other move.
Article Summary: The Pittsburgh Steelers still do not know who will lead the offense. Aaron Rodgers remains undecided about returning, despite a steady 2025 season. Will Howard, now in his second year, has drawn internal support. Ty Simpson has quietly built a case as a potential option.
